name: Handala
nexus: Iran (MOIS — Ministry of Intelligence and Security)
ttps: T1486 — Data Encrypted for Impact (destructive wipe via MDM)
T1072 — Software Deployment Tools (Microsoft Intune MDM abuse)
T1059 — Command and Scripting Interpreter
T0826 — Loss of Availability (manufacturing/shipping disruption)
Hacktivist information operations — faketivist cover for state operations
Hybrid cyber-physical: cartel recruitment for physical violence against targets
notes: MARCH 26 UPDATE: Executed Lockheed Martin data breach TODAY — leaked engineer data 6 days after 4 domain seizures and days after leadership decapitation. Autonomous cell model confirmed fully operational. 4 domain seizures + 2 leadership killings = did NOT halt operations. Stryker MDM wiper March 11. FBI domains seized March 20. Lockheed breach March 26. Telegram still active. IOCs: handala-hack[.]to, handala-redwanted[.]to, justicehomeland[.]org, karmabelow80[.]org (SEIZED), Handala_Team@outlook[.]com.

name: VOLTZITE
ttps: T0886 - Remote Services exploitation via VPN/edge device zero-days (Fortinet, Cisco, NETGEAR, Zyxel)
T0817 - Drive-by Compromise via ICS vendor portals
T0819 - Exploit Public-Facing Application (Siemens S7 series vulnerabilities)
T0843 - Program Download to modify PLC ladder logic
T0836 - Modify Control Logic in Siemens/GE PLCs
T0840 - Network Connection Enumeration via OPC-UA scanning (port 4840)
T0800 - Activate Firmware Update Mode for persistence
Living off the land (LOTL) — no custom malware, native tools only
notes: Most significant China-nexus OT threat. Confirmed: exfiltrating operational/sensor data from U.S. energy and pipeline networks. Confirmed: manipulating engineering workstations to determine what process conditions would trigger shutdowns. SYLVANITE provides initial footholds; VOLTZITE conducts deeper OT operations. Long-dwell, LOTL tradecraft makes detection extremely difficult. Intelligence confidence elevated following Dragos 2026 YIR and corroborating CISA attribution.

name: ELECTRUM
ttps: T0817 - Drive-by Compromise targeting utility sector employees
T0862 - Supply Chain Compromise via ICS software update mechanisms
T0832 - Manipulation of View — HMI display falsification
T0881 - Service Stop targeting protection relays
T0813 - Denial of Control via SCADA flooding attacks
T0840 - Network Connection Enumeration via OPC-UA scanning
ASPX webshells on Exchange servers
Low-frequency DNS anomalies for C2 communication
notes: Responsible for 2015/2016 Ukraine power grid attacks and CRASHOVERRIDE/Industroyer malware. Targeted distributed energy systems in Poland (late 2025) with deliberate operational impact attempts. APRIL 1 UPDATE: Dragos April 1 OT Threat Landscape blog confirms 'continued activity by ELECTRUM, KAMACITE, VOLTZITE, and BAUXITE' — status updated from SILENT to ACTIVE. Previously assessed as covert pre-positioning Feb 28-Apr 1. Watch for: ASPX webshells on Exchange servers, low-frequency DNS anomalies. Group likely building capability for disruption operations tied to geopolitical timeline.

name: KAMACITE
ttps: T0865 - Spearphishing Attachment targeting OT engineers (NERC CIP-themed lures)
T0866 - Exploitation of Remote Services (Citrix, VPN)
T0846 - Remote System Discovery via PLC enumeration
T0863 - User Execution via malicious engineering project files
T0853 - Scripting exploitation of automation platforms
T0877 - I/O Module Discovery for impact planning
Historian server vulnerability exploitation for process intelligence gathering
notes: Initial access and enablement group — establishes persistent footholds for handoff to ELECTRUM. Recent campaign targeting North American electric utilities uses spearphishing lures themed around NERC CIP compliance documentation and ICS vendor security bulletins. Demonstrated specific targeting of GE Vernova EMS platforms and Honeywell Experion DCS. APRIL 1 UPDATE: Dragos confirms continued KAMACITE activity alongside ELECTRUM — status updated from PREPOSITIONING to ACTIVE.

name: SYLVANITE
ttps: T0817 - Drive-by Compromise via exposed industrial HMIs
T0806 - Brute Force against internet-facing OT/SCADA interfaces and VPNs
T0819 - Exploit Public-Facing Application (edge devices)
T0804 - Block Reporting Message to suppress detection
Establishing persistent footholds on edge devices for VOLTZITE handoff
notes: China-nexus initial access broker — confirmed in Dragos 2026 OT/ICS Year in Review. SYLVANITE establishes footholds via edge device exploitation and hands them to VOLTZITE for deeper OT operations. The SYLVANITE → VOLTZITE handoff model indicates operational security discipline and division of labor. Disrupting SYLVANITE's initial access may be more tractable than hunting VOLTZITE post-handoff. NOTE: Some demo data incorrectly attributed SYLVANITE as 'Unknown/Criminal' — Dragos attribution is China-nexus.

name: PYROXENE
ttps: T0865 - Spearphishing Attachment with ICS-themed lures
T0877 - I/O Module Discovery targeting PLCs
T0806 - Brute Force authentication against engineering workstations
T0836 - Modify Control Logic targeting safety instrumented systems (SIS)
T0856 - Spoof Reporting Message to hide process manipulation
Hacktivist-aligned information operations and screenshot sharing
notes: Execution phase confirmed. Newly identified in Dragos 2026 YIR. Iran (MOIS) affiliation — overlaps with APT33/Magnallium activity clusters. Status elevated from SILENT to ACTIVE following Iran-US conflict escalation (Feb 28, 2026 U.S. strikes). Previously targeted UAE petrochemical facility. Demonstrated interest in safety instrumented systems (SIS) for potential sabotage capability. IDF struck Iranian energy infrastructure and killed Intel Minister Khatib on March 18 — Iran-US conflict escalation confirmed destructive execution phase (Stryker/Intune). Assess PYROXENE and PAPAGALLO elevated from pre-positioning to potential execution posture — pending Dragos confirmation. 7-14 day retaliation window active following MOIS Director Khatib killing and South Pars energy strikes.

name: AZURITE
ttps: T0862 - Supply Chain Compromise via ERP/MES vendor software
T0817 - Drive-by Compromise via industrial software forums
T0832 - Manipulation of View in MES reporting systems
T0846 - Remote System Discovery targeting manufacturing networks
T0852 - Screen Capture for IP theft from engineering workstations
T0849 - Masquerading as legitimate ICS vendor processes
notes: APRIL 1 DISCLOSURE: Dragos publicly disclosed AZURITE as a new OT threat group on April 1, 2026. Targets OT engineering workstations for operational data exfiltration and long-term persistent access. Leverages internet-facing infrastructure vulnerabilities for initial access. Status updated to ACTIVE per Dragos disclosure. Origin/nexus pending further Dragos attribution reporting.

name: Cyber Islamic Resistance
ttps: DDoS
Reconnaissance
Website defacement
notes: New group identified in Resecurity multi-domain phase analysis (March 24, 2026). Conducting DDoS, recon, and defacement synchronized with kinetic operations. Low-to-medium impact tier individually — threat is volume and coordination with other groups creating attacker-favorable noise.

name: Fatimion Cyber Team
ttps: T1498 - Network Denial of Service (DDoS)
T1491.002 - External Defacement
T1190 - Exploit Public-Facing Application (SQL injection)
T1041 - Data Exfiltration (PII leaks)
T1485 - Data Destruction / Wiper Malware [Unit 42, March 26]
notes: Also known as FAD Team and Iran Resistance Hub. Significant TTP upgrade: Unit 42 (March 26) now assesses Fatimion/FAD Team as focused on WIPER MALWARE and permanent data destruction - major escalation beyond earlier DDoS/defacement characterization. Confirmed targets: Kuwait News Agency (DDoS), Bahrain News Agency (disabled), Qatari oil firm Gasco, Qatar Radio. Global SQL injection campaign confirmed by Flashpoint with PII leaks from multiple countries. Part of Operation Epic Fury response coalition. Synchronized with physical operations per Resecurity.

name: Cyber Fattah
ttps: T1498 — Network Denial of Service (DDoS campaigns)
T1491.002 — Defacement: External Defacement (website defacement)
T1595 — Active Scanning (reconnaissance against CI targets)
T1592 — Gather Victim Host Information (target profiling)
T1041 — Exfiltration Over C2 Channel (data theft — confirmed GNET March 23)
notes: MARCH 27 UPDATE: Energy sector targeting confirmed — Industrial Cyber reports Cyber Fattah attacks detected against energy infrastructure in Jordan (same country as today's confirmed water treatment ICS Level 1 breach; potential connection unconfirmed). Self-identifies as 'Iranian Cyber Team.' Operates alongside Cyber Islamic Resistance and Fatimion Cyber Team as part of coordinated Iran-aligned hacktivist coalition. TTPs updated with MITRE ATT&CK IDs based on Resecurity, GNET, and Industrial Cyber sourcing (March 23-27, 2026). Individual impact tier: low-to-medium. Coalition impact: higher via noise-layer coverage of deeper operations.

name: Dragonfly
ttps: T0839 Module Firmware
T0836 Modify Parameter
T0857 System Firmware
notes: CERT-PL + Team Cymru (March 30, 2026): Hard brick of Hitachi RTU560 on Polish power grid via default creds + corrupted ELF firmware. Moxa NPort lockout in same campaign. Dragos compares case 3 TTP to TRISIS/TRITON class — I/O falsification + persistence.

name: BAUXITE
ttps: T0819 - Exploit Public-Facing Application (internet-exposed OT devices)
T0877 - I/O Module Discovery targeting PLCs and HMIs
T0862 - Supply Chain Compromise
Internet-exposed PLC/HMI exploitation
notes: APRIL 1 DISCLOSURE: Confirmed active by Dragos April 1, 2026 OT Threat Landscape blog alongside ELECTRUM, KAMACITE, and VOLTZITE. Iran-nexus group targeting internet-exposed OT devices — PLCs, HMIs, engineering workstations. IOCONTROL/CyberAv3ngers-adjacent TTPs. Previously identified in Dragos 2025 reporting as targeting internet-accessible OT/ICS devices in critical infrastructure sectors. Attribution confidence MEDIUM — awaiting further Dragos reporting.
